text:
Once upon a time, in a valley filled with whispering leaves, there was a peculiar owl named Oliver Owl. Oliver was famous among the animal inhabitants of the valley for his peculiar hobby: every full moon, he would seek out the oldest oak tree in the woods to meditate under its dense branches, hoping to receive enlightening dreams about the future.

One night, under the oak that had witnessed countless full moons, Oliver had a dream so vivid it felt like living history. In his dream, he was sailing on moonlit waters across a lake adorned with ghostly shadows of trees dancing in the starlight. He encountered a mysterious mermaid with iridescent scales and eyes like jewels.

The mermaid welcomed Oliver onto her shell-boat fashioned from a hollowed-out tree trunk. "Oh daring owl," she sang, her voice like tinkling bells, "Why do you journey so far away into the night? You seek secrets?"

Oliver blinked owlishly. "Yes, something
